name: Nana Buruku
description: Nana Buruku, also known as Nana Bukuu, is a powerful and ancient deity revered in Yoruba and Fon traditions, particularly in West Africa (Nigeria, Benin, Togo). She is considered the mother of the earth and the creator of humanity, often associated with the primordial waters and the transition between life and death. Nana Buruku is a complex figure, embodying both nurturing and destructive aspects. She is the guardian of tradition, justice, and the sacred feminine. Her power is linked to the earth, the swamps, and the mysteries of the ancestors. She is often depicted as an old woman, representing wisdom and experience. Nana Buruku is believed to possess the power to heal, protect, and bring prosperity, but she can also inflict punishment on those who disrespect her or violate the laws of nature. She is associated with the Adan tree, which is considered sacred to her. Her worship often involves rituals and offerings performed in groves and near bodies of water. Nana Buruku is a significant figure in understanding the cosmology and moral values of the Yoruba and Fon people. She embodies the cyclical nature of life, the importance of respecting the earth, and the power of ancestral wisdom. She is seen as the embodiment of the Great Mother, representing the origins of all things and the enduring connection between humanity and the natural world. She is a powerful force, respected and feared in equal measure.
subtitle: The Great Mother, Ancient Creator

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Humanity
keyMyths.description: Nana Buruku is credited with creating humanity from clay, shaping them with her own hands and breathing life into them.

keyMyths.title: Nana Buruku and Smallpox
keyMyths.description: Nana Buruku is sometimes associated with smallpox, both as a bringer of the disease and as the one who can cure it, highlighting her dual nature.

keyMyths.title: The Sacred Grove
keyMyths.description: Nana Buruku's sacred groves are places of worship and connection to the ancestors, where rituals and offerings are performed to honor her.
